chapter_context.summary: Describes Daniel's three-week mourning and fasting by the river Tigris, his vision of a majestic heavenly being clothed in linen, and the angel's explanation of the spiritual warfare in the heavenly realms involving the prince of Persia and Michael.

outline.text: Daniel's Fasting and the Vision of the Glorious Linen-Clothed Man by Hiddekel
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Daniel's Fasting and the Vision of the Glorious Linen-Clothed Man by Hiddekel (10:1-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Daniel.

outline.text: The Angel's Touch: Spiritual Warfare with the Prince of Persia
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Angel's Touch: Spiritual Warfare with the Prince of Persia (10:10-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Daniel.

outline.text: Daniel Strengthened and Informed of the Upcoming Geopolitical Conflicts
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Daniel Strengthened and Informed of the Upcoming Geopolitical Conflicts (10:15-21), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Daniel.

key_themes.title: The Sovereignty of God Over Kingdoms
key_themes.description: A central message of Daniel is that God rules in the kingdom of men, setting up and deposing kings according to His eternal purpose.

key_themes.title: Faithfulness under Pressure
key_themes.description: Daniel and his friends demonstrate unwavering allegiance to God's law in the face of compromise, death, and exile.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the faithfulness and courage demonstrated in Chapter 10, how can you stand firm in your convictions when faced with cultural and societal pressures to compromise?
In what ways does the vision of God's ultimate eternal kingdom in this chapter bring hope and stability to your daily life?

cultural_historical.title: Babylonian and Persian Courts
cultural_historical.text: Daniel was trained in the literature and language of the Chaldeans, serving as a high-ranking official under both Babylonian and Medo-Persian administrations, illustrating the historical reality of the Jewish diaspora.
